message:
fuzz: add fuzz_polynomial_metrics target with numeric invariants

New structure-aware fuzz target exercising:

- chromatic_polynomial / tutte_polynomial
- s_metric
- floyd_warshall
- wiener_index, wiener_index_weighted (the new variants from the
  recent Rust port)
- global_efficiency
- flow_hierarchy_directed
- rich_club_coefficient

Beyond the no-panic invariant, asserts these runtime contracts:

Polynomial family
  - chromatic_polynomial(G, 0) == 0 for non-empty G.
  - chromatic_polynomial(G, 1) == 0 when |E| > 0 (no proper
    1-coloring once an edge appears).
  - Both polynomials produce identical values when called twice
    on the same graph at the same evaluation point (no hidden
    randomness).

s_metric
  - Equals sum of deg(u) * deg(v) over the edge set, recomputed
    independently from the graph.

Floyd-Warshall family
  - Diagonal is zero.
  - Symmetry on undirected: d[u][v] == d[v][u].
  - Triangle inequality: d[u][w] <= d[u][v] + d[v][w] for every
    triple of reachable nodes.

Wiener family
  - wiener_index_weighted with explicit unit weights == wiener_index.
  - Both share their finite/infinite status.

Global efficiency / flow hierarchy
  - Both lie in [0, 1] modulo float epsilon.

Rich-club coefficient
  - All coefficients are non-negative.

Includes a coverage-minimized seed corpus of 220 inputs harvested
from a 60s libfuzzer pass + merge=1 minimization (2513 cov edges,
7043 features). Target was registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml, built
clean, ran 7868+ iterations (~60s) without invariant violations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+doc = false
